scholarships.name: HEC Overseas PhD Scholarship
scholarships.hostUniversities: Top-ranked universities worldwide (USA, UK, Germany, Australia, Canada, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly living allowance ($1,500-$2,000), return airfare, health insurance, and HEC attestation support for Pakistani PhD students.
benefits: Full tuition fees at the host university
Monthly living allowance $1,500-$2,000 depending on country
eligibility: Pakistani citizen with valid CNIC
Master's degree with minimum CGPA 3.0/4.0
Valid IELTS (6.5+) or TOEFL (80+) score
Age limit: 40 years on application deadline
Not already enrolled in a PhD program abroad
HEC attested degrees
GRE/GAT subject score where applicable
documentsRequired: No objection certificate from employer (if applicable)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open January and close end of March. Check hec.gov.pk.

scholarships.name: KAUST Fellowship (Saudi Arabia)
scholarships.provider: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
scholarships.hostUniversities: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly living stipend ($20,000-$30,000/year), on-campus housing, relocation support, and health insurance for Masters/PhD in STEM fields.
eligibility: Outstanding academic record (top 5% of class)
Bachelor's degree in STEM field for Masters
Master's degree for PhD
Strong English proficiency
Research experience preferred
Not already a KAUST student
scholarships.deadlineNote: Rolling admissions; priority deadline typically January 15 for fall entry.

scholarships.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master Degree (EMJMD) - European Union
scholarships.hostUniversities: Consortium of 2-3 European universities per programme (e.g. 200+ programmes across EU)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly living allowance (€1,400/month), travel costs, installation costs, and health insurance for a joint master's programme studied across 2-3 EU countries.
benefits: Health insurance for the entire duration
scholarships.eligibility: Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in a relevant field. English proficiency (IELTS 6.5+ / TOEFL 90+). Not previously awarded an Erasmus Mundus scholarship. Meet specific programme requirements.
documentsRequired: Online application via the EMJMD website
scholarships.deadlineNote: Most programmes have deadlines between October and January. Check individual programme pages on eacea.ec.europa.eu.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Research or work experience (varies by programme)

scholarships.name: Australian Research Training Program (RTP)
scholarships.hostUniversities: All Australian universities participating in the RTP scheme
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition offset, living stipend (AUD $32,000-$40,000/year), and health insurance for research-based Masters and PhD in Australia.
eligibility: International student (non-Australian citizen/permanent resident)
Strong academic record (Honors 1 or equivalent for PhD; Honors 2A for Masters)
Meet the entry requirements of the chosen university and research degree
Have a supervisory agreement with a potential supervisor
Not already holding an equivalent research degree
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadlines vary by university; most round 1 deadlines are around September-October for the following year. Some universities have rolling admissions.

scholarships.name: Norwegian Government Scholarship for Developing Countries
scholarships.provider: Norwegian Government / Norwegian State Educational Loan Fund (Lånekassen)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Norwegian public universities (University of Oslo, NTNU, UiB, UiT, NMBU, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition (free at public universities), living stipend (NOK 151,000-162,000/year), travel grant, and health insurance for students from developing countries.
benefits: No tuition fees at Norwegian public universities
Travel grant (depending on country of origin)
Health insurance through Norwegian National Insurance Scheme
No tuition fees even for international students
eligibility: Citizen of a developing country on the DAC list
Admitted to a full-degree Masters or PhD programme at a Norwegian public university
English proficiency (TOEFL 80+ / IELTS 6.0+)
Meet the specific admission requirements of the chosen university
Not already have a degree from a Norwegian institution
documentsRequired: Online application through the Norwegian university portal
scholarships.deadlineNote: University admissions portal opens October 1 and closes December 1. Some universities have separate deadlines for international applicants.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Bachelor's or Master's degree in relevant field

scholarships.name: Italian Government Scholarship (Invest Your Talent in Italy)
scholarships.provider: Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Italian universities and AFAM institutions (Politecnico di Milano, Sapienza, Bocconi, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition waiver, monthly stipend (€900/month), health insurance, and Italian language course for international students in Italy.
eligibility: Citizen of eligible countries (check MAECI website)
Bachelor's degree for Masters; Master's for PhD
English proficiency (B2 level or higher)
Age limit: 28 years for Masters, 40 for PhD
Not already residing in Italy
Meet university-specific admission requirements
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open in April and close in May. Results announced July-August.

scholarships.name: Belgium Scholarships for Developing Countries (ARES)
scholarships.provider: Academy of Research and Higher Education (ARES), Belgium
scholarships.hostUniversities: Belgian universities and colleges (UCLouvain, KU Leuven, UGent, ULB, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly living stipend (€1,170/month), travel costs, visa fees, accommodation support, and health insurance for students from developing countries.
eligibility: Citizenship of an ARES partner developing country (including Pakistan)
Bachelor's degree for Masters; Master's for PhD
Professional experience of at least 2 years (for Masters)
Not already holding a degree from a Belgian institution
Proficient in French or English depending on the programme
Under 40 years of age for Masters, 45 for PhD
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open in November and close in February. Check ares-ac.be.

scholarships.name: ANSO Scholarship for Young Talents (China)
scholarships.provider: Alliance of National Science Foundations of Asia (ANSO)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) institutes and partner universities
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly stipend (CNY 6,000-7,000/month for PhD, CNY 3,000 for Masters), accommodation, and health insurance through CAS institutes.
benefits: Monthly stipend: Masters CNY 3,000, PhD CNY 7,000
eligibility: Non-Chinese citizen under 35 years old
Bachelor's degree for Masters; Master's for PhD
Strong academic record and research potential
English proficiency (or Chinese proficiency)
Not already enrolled in a Chinese institution
Endorsed by a CAS supervisor
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open in November and close in mid-February.

scholarships.name: Singapore International Graduate Award (SINGA)
scholarships.provider: Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), Singapore
scholarships.hostUniversities: National University of Singapore (NUS), Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD), Singapore Management University (SMU), Duke-NUS Medical School
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly stipend (SGD $2,200-$2,700), one-time airfare, settlement allowance, and visa fees for PhD in Singapore's top research universities.
benefits: Monthly stipend SGD $2,200-$2,700 (increases over PhD years)
eligibility: Non-Singaporean citizen
Strong academic record (upper second class honours or better)
Bachelor's or Master's in STEM field
Good English proficiency
Not already holding a doctoral degree
Research experience preferred
scholarships.deadlineNote: Two main rounds: January 1 deadline for August intake, June 1 deadline for January intake.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Research interest alignment with A*STAR supervisors

scholarships.name: CAS-TWAS President's Fellowship (PhD in China)
scholarships.provider: Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) and The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly stipend (CNY 7,000/month), accommodation, and travel support for PhD study at CAS institutes for developing country students.
eligibility: Citizenship of a TWAS member developing country (including Pakistan)
Under 35 years of age
Master's degree in a relevant field
Strong academic and research record
English proficiency
Not a Chinese citizen
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications open in October and close in mid-February.

scholarships.name: Korean Government Scholarship Program (GKS)
scholarships.provider: National Institute for International Education (NIIED), South Korea
scholarships.hostUniversities: Top Korean universities (Seoul National University, KAIST, Yonsei, Korea University, POSTECH, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly stipend (KRW 1,000,000/month), round-trip airfare, settlement allowance, Korean language training, and health insurance for Masters/PhD in South Korea.
eligibility: Citizenship of a GKS partner country (173 countries including Pakistan)
Under 40 years of age
Bachelor's degree for Masters; Master's for PhD
GPA above 80% (or 2.64/4.0)
Not a Korean citizen
Healthy enough to pursue studies
Previously not enrolled at a Korean university
documentsRequired: GKS university acceptance letter (if applying via university track)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Embassy track: typically February-March. University track: varies by university (usually February-April).
scholarships.durationNote: Masters 2 years + 1 year Korean language; PhD 3 years + 1 year Korean language

scholarships.name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarship (ESKAT)
scholarships.provider: Swiss Confederation / State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation (SERI)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Swiss universities (ETH Zurich, EPFL, University of Zurich, University of Geneva, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly stipend (CHF 1,920/month), flight ticket, health insurance, and Swiss public transport pass for PhD/postdoc in Switzerland.
benefits: Full tuition fees (most Swiss universities charge low fees anyway)
eligibility: Citizenship of a country on the SERI list (including Pakistan)
Under 35 years of age for PhD; under 35 for postdoc
Master's degree with excellent academic record
Admitted or applying to a Swiss university
Not already residing in Switzerland
Research proposal accepted by a Swiss professor
documentsRequired: Letter of acceptance from Swiss professor
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open in August and close in December. Results announced March-April.

scholarships.name: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
scholarships.hostUniversities: University of Toronto, McGill University, Arizona State University, University of Edinburgh, Sciences Po, University of Cape Town, and 60+ partner institutions
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, accommodation, books, living expenses, mentoring, leadership development, and transition support for young Africans and underserved communities.
eligibility: Primarily African youth from economically disadvantaged backgrounds
Academically talented and high-achieving
Service-oriented with leadership potential
Financial need demonstrated
Meets partner institution admission requirements
Not already hold a degree from a partner institution
documentsRequired: Proof of admission to partner institution
scholarships.deadlineNote: Each partner institution sets its own deadlines. Apply directly through the chosen partner institution.

scholarships.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master - Leadership in Digital Transformation
scholarships.hostUniversities: Aalborg University (Denmark), University of Trento (Italy), Wroclaw University (Poland)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly allowance €1,400, travel and installation costs for a joint master's in digital transformation across 3 EU countries.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science, business, or related field
English proficiency (IELTS 6.5+ / TOEFL 90+)
Not previously received an Erasmus Mundus scholarship
EU or non-EU citizens eligible
scholarships.deadlineNote: Most EMJMD programmes close between October and January.

scholarships.name: Türkiye Burslari (Turkish Government Scholarship)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Turkish state universities (Istanbul University, METU, Bilkent, Boğaziçi, Sabancı, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, monthly stipend ($700-$950), round-trip airfare, accommodation, health insurance, and 1 year Turkish language training for all degree levels.
eligibility: Non-Turkish citizen
Not already studying in Turkey
Under 21 for Bachelors, under 30 for Masters, under 35 for PhD
Minimum GPA 75% (for Bachelors 75%, Masters/PhD 80%)
Healthy enough to study
Not previously received a Türkiye Burslari scholarship
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications open in January and close in February 20. Results announced in July-August.
scholarships.durationNote: Bachelors 4 years; Masters 2 years; PhD 3-4 years (plus 1 year Turkish language)

scholarships.name: New Zealand Scholarships (Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships)
scholarships.provider: New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade (MFAT)
scholarships.hostUniversities: New Zealand universities (University of Auckland, Victoria University of Wellington, University of Otago, etc.)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full tuition, living allowance (NZD $400/week), establishment allowance, travel costs, and health insurance for students from eligible developing countries.
eligibility: Citizen of an eligible developing country (including Pakistan)
Under 40 years of age
Bachelor's degree with good grades
Not already a NZ citizen/permanent resident
Meet university admission requirements
Not previously studied in NZ for 12+ months
IELTS 6.5+ or TOEFL 90+
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open in February and close in March. Check scholarships.mfat.govt.nz.
